issue.title: Several issues with the mailing list (librelist), time to look for an alternative?
issue.body:
There are several issues currently with the mailing list on librelist:

0. The website (http://librelist.com/) is currently down. This might be transient, or this might be because the service has been slowly decaying over the last months.

1. The synchro to http://flask.pocoo.org/mailinglist/archive/ is broken since 2012/10/29.

2. I've been unsubscribed a few months ago due to transient a problem with my DNS. I have since tried many times to subscribe again with the same address, with no success. This may be a very specific problem, or this may be a manifestation of more general issues with the mailing list management system.

3. I have also tried registering with gmail adresses created just for the purpose of reading the Flask mailing list. I've had mixed successes, and I've also noticed that some of the emails I could see on the librelist.com archives were not delivered to my account(s).

4. AFAICT, traffic to the mailing list has been going down over approximately the last 2-3 months. This might be because interest in Flask is slowly fading away (if true, bad news for us Flask users), or this might be because of some of the technical issues I've mentioned above (or others), in which case proactive actions should be taken to keep users engaged.

For these reasons, I'm wondering if moving to another mailing list management platform would not be appropriate.

  S.

comment.body: Just for anyone who stumbles across this later, we've now migrated the mailing list to https://mail.python.org/mailman/listinfo/flask/

issue.title: Using error log level does not show SSL fingerprint dialog
issue.body:
I had upgraded from 1.7.2 to 1.7.3 stable on Mac OS X 10.10.3, started it as server, then Windows 8.1. as client. 

Server reported in a loop:
~~~
NOTE: log level: NOTE
NOTE: log file: /var/log/synergy.log
NOTE: started server, waiting for clients
2015-05-29 07:47:42.899 synergys[28316:8758894] starting cocoa loop
NOTE: accepted client connection
NOTE: new client disconnected
NOTE: accepted client connection
NOTE: new client disconnected
...repeat
~~~

Checking the client on Windows:
~~~
NOTE: log level: ERROR
NOTE: log file: C:/Program Files/Synergy/log/synergy.log
activeDesktop:Default
ERROR: failed to verify server certificate fingerprint
activeDesktop:Default
ERROR: failed to verify server certificate fingerprint
activeDesktop:Default
...repeat
~~~

Because error log level set below at a verbosity less than NOTE in my previous 1.7.2 installation, and I knew that WARNING or ERROR level logging seems to break functionality on Synergy in unexpected ways, I then set the Windows client log level to NOTE.

I was rewarded with a warning dialog box that noted the server certificate was unknown, and did I want to allow it (paraphrasing, I did not capture this screen). Upon accepting it, Synergy then was able to connect. It appears that the **log level affects whether the critical dialog box appears** when establishing the SSL (?) connection, otherwise loop continues forever with no visible reason why unless the log level is changed back to NOTE or higher verbosity. 

Related Issues:
* Unfortunately, I still need to restart Synergy regularly due to longstanding bug [#451](https://github.com/synergy/synergy/issues/451)  (mouse speed doubling on windows client with mac server upon sleep). This requires a functioning GUI, but the GUI works only if log level is set to NOTE or greater verbosity. [#4466] 
* Leaving error logging in NOTE level, however, seemed to cause system lockup when the log became very large over the course of a day or two, (this may have been fixed in 1.7.3), so high verbosity logging was undesirable. However, setting the log level to a lower verbosity triggers bug [#4466]
* Workaround is to QUIT Synergy and then relaunch it every time the mouse-doubling occurs. If the server computer goes to sleep after 5 minutes of inactivity, which is common, restart is necessary frequently.
